title: Ensure that the admission control plugin EventRateLimit is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin EventRateLimit is set
input_row.description: Limit the rate at which the API server accepts requests.
input_row.rationale: Using EventRateLimit admission control enforces a limit on the number of events that the API Server will accept in a given time slice. A misbehaving workload could overwhelm and DoS the API Server, making it unavailable. This particularly applies to a multi-tenant cluster, where there might be a small percentage of misbehaving tenants which could have a significant impact on the performance of the cluster overall. Hence, it is recommended to limit the rate of events that the API server will accept. Impact: You need to carefully tune in limits as per your environment.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --enable-admission-plugins argument is set to a value that includes EventRateLimit.
input_row.remediation: Follow the Kubernetes documentation and set the desired limits in a configuration file. Then, edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml and set the below parameters. --enable-admission-plugins=...,EventRateLimit,... --admission-control-config-file=<path/to/configuration/file> Default Value: By default, EventRateLimit is not set. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/command-line-tools-reference/kube- apiserver/ 2. https://kubernetes.io/docs/reference/access-authn-authz/admission- controllers/#eventratelimit 3. https://github.com/staebler/community/blob/9873b632f4d99b5d99c38c9b15fe2f8 b93d0a746/contributors/design- proposals/admission_control_event_rate_limit.md
input_row.impact: You need to carefully tune in limits as per your environment.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_eventratelimit_admission_plugin_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **Objective:** Ensure the Kubernetes API server is configured with the EventRateLimit admission control plugin enabled and that the required configuration file is specified.
- **Kubernetes API/CLI Methods:**
  - Use `kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o json` to retrieve the API server pod specification (on clusters using static pods, typically self-hosted or kubeadm-based).
  - Alternatively, inspect the file `/etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml` if direct node access is available (for static pod deployments).
- **Configuration Properties to Validate:**
  - The `--enable-admission-plugins` flag includes `EventRateLimit`.
  - The `--admission-control-config-file` flag is set and points to a valid configuration file.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** The API server manifest or running pod spec includes `--enable-admission-plugins` with `EventRateLimit` listed, and `--admission-control-config-file` is present.
  - **Fail:** Either `EventRateLimit` is missing from `--enable-admission-plugins`, or `--admission-control-config-file` is not set.
- **Example kubectl Command:**
  ```sh
  kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o json | jq -r '.items[].spec.containers[].command[]' | grep -- '--enable-admission-plugins'
  kubectl -n kube-system get pod -l component=kube-apiserver -o json | jq -r '.items[].spec.containers[].command[]' | grep -- '--admission-control-config-file'
  ```
- **Expected Output:**
  - `--enable-admission-plugins=...,EventRateLimit,...`
  - `--admission-control-config-file=<path/to/configuration/file>`
- **Remediation Guidance:** If either flag is missing or misconfigured, update the API server manifest to include both as per remediation instructions.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11.1 PDF
